raw_input: Therapists spend 2 hours on notes after every session. What if they didn't have to?
value_proposition: Reclaim 2 hours per day by letting AI handle your clinical documentation while you focus on what matters—your patients.
pain_point: Therapists are drowning in post-session documentation, losing 2+ hours daily to administrative work instead of patient care, self-care, or simply going home on time.
desired_action: Sign up for a free trial to experience automated note generation from their next session.
tone_direction: Empathetic yet urgent. Acknowledge the exhaustion and validate the struggle, then offer hope. Conversational, not corporate. Think 'colleague who gets it' rather than 'tech company selling something.' Use second-person ('you') to make it personal.
visual_direction: Contrast-driven storytelling: BEFORE shows a therapist alone in dim office lighting at 8pm, slumped over a laptop with session notes, coffee cup, tired eyes. AFTER shows the same therapist leaving the office in daylight, relaxed, phone in hand showing JotPsych completion notification. For video: time-lapse of clock spinning from 6pm to 8pm while therapist types, then hard cut to JotPsych interface auto-generating notes in 3 minutes. Color palette: warm, calming blues and greens (trust/healthcare) with pops of energizing coral for CTAs.
key_phrases: What if you didn't have to?
Your session ends. Your notes don't have to.
Built by clinicians who've been there
